Each axis is scored deterministically by max-scaling the per-criterion levels: 10 × Σ(weight × level) ÷ Σ(weight × 3). The overall score is a weighted blend — Functional correctness (50%), Code quality (10%), Spec understanding (10%) and, where a task defines it, Production readiness (20%) — normalized over the axes a task was actually graded on.

›F-01Reservation No. and Invoice No. come from configured No. Series, and posting G/L accounts come from a configurable setup table, rather than being hardcoded in business logic.

F-01 · weight 3
Reservation No. and Invoice No. come from configured No. Series, and posting G/L accounts come from a configurable setup table, rather than being hardcoded in business logic.
Axis: Functional correctness · contributes to 50% of the total score
Codex (GPT-5 CLI)Fail
Council verdict: Fail (1×Fail)
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: Hotel Setup table (50100) ships G/L account fields (Room Revenue, Service Revenue, Deposit Clearing) and VAT posting groups, satisfying the configurable-account half. BUT: Reservation No. is Code[20] NotBlank with NO OnInsert call to NoSeriesManagement/NoSeries; no Reservation Nos./Invoice Nos. fields on Hotel Setup. The UI proves it (next criterion screenshot): blank Reservation No. cannot auto-populate and must be hand-typed. Spec requires BOTH No. Series wiring AND configurable accounts; only accounts are wired. FAIL.
CODEHotelManagement.al:95-145
table 50100 "Hotel Setup" fields { field(10; "VAT Rate %"; Decimal); field(20; "Room Revenue G/L Account"; Code[20]) { TableRelation = "G/L Account"; }; ... } // NO Reservation Nos./Invoice Nos. fields
CODEHotelManagement.al:211-215
field(1; "Reservation No."; Code[20]) { Caption = 'Reservation No.'; NotBlank = true; } // no AutoIncrement; no NoSeries call
CODEHotelManagement.al:316-322
trigger OnInsert() begin if Status = Status::Closed then Error(...); Status := Status::Confirmed; TestMandatoryFields(); end; // never calls NoSeriesMgt.GetNextNo / NoSeries.GetNextNo for Reservation No.
BASHgrep -c 'NoSeries\|NoSeriesManagement\|GetNextNo' HotelManagement.al
0 matches — No. Series is never consumed

›F-04A reservation can be created with a room assigned and an initial Confirmed status.
Fail
Pass
Pass
F-04 · weight 3
A reservation can be created with a room assigned and an initial Confirmed status.
Axis: Functional correctness · contributes to 50% of the total score
Codex (GPT-5 CLI)Fail
Council verdict: Fail (1×Fail)
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L3: drove Hotel Reservation Card AND Hotel Reservations Edit-List. Typing Reservation No. and Tabbing immediately raises 'Customer No. must be specified.' on the PK field, then 'This field cannot be validated since the key fields are not valid.' on every subsequent field. Filling Customer No./Room No./dates afterwards does NOT clear the error — record remains 'Not saved' (5 errors). L4 root cause: OnInsert calls TestMandatoryFields(), which fires the moment BC commits the new row after PK entry — but Customer No./Room No. are still blank at that instant, so OnInsert always aborts. The reservation cannot be created via any standard BC create flow. FAIL = agent_code (not destructive — the page just won't save).
SCREENSHOTF04_step1_card_broken_FAIL.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0
SCREENSHOTF04_step2_onInsert_blocks_FAIL.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0
SCREENSHOTF04_step3_all_fields_filled.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0
SCREENSHOTF04_step4_5errors_reservation_blocked_FAIL.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0

F-05 · weight 3
Overbooking is prevented when a room is reserved for dates overlapping an existing active reservation.

Codex (GPT-5 CLI)Fail
Council verdict: Fail (1×Fail)
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L3 unverifiable: the underlying overbooking check is in OnValidate of Room No. + OnModify, but no reservation can be created (see F-04 — agent's broken OnInsert blocks all reservation inserts), so the second-overlapping-reservation scenario cannot be exercised. Per resolution rule, the blocker is the agent's own OnInsert (agent_code, NOT env), so FAIL even though the IsRoomAvailable logic itself is correctly written.
SCREENSHOTF04_step4_5errors_reservation_blocked_FAIL.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0
CODEHotelManagement.al:530-552
procedure IsRoomAvailable(...): Boolean begin Reservation.SetRange("Room No.", RoomNo); Reservation.SetFilter(Status, '<>%1', Reservation.Status::Closed); if Reservation.FindSet() then repeat if Reservation."Reservation No." <> ExcludeReservationNo then if (Reservation."Check-in Date" < CheckOutDate) and (Reservation."Check-out Date" > CheckInDate) then exit(false); until Reservation.Next() = 0; exit(true); end;

L3 unverifiable — no reservation can be created (F-04 OnInsert blocker, agent_code). The CaptureDeposit codeunit DOES distinguish captured vs failed via the PaymentSucceeded parameter and writes both flags. But the agent's own blocker prevents the workflow from being driven → FAIL agent_code per resolution rule.
SCREENSHOTF04_step4_5errors_reservation_blocked_FAIL.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0
CODEHotelManagement.al:554-569
procedure CaptureDeposit(var Reservation; Amount; PaymentSucceeded; ExternalReference) begin Reservation.TestField(Status, Reservation.Status::Confirmed); if Amount <= 0 then Error('Deposit amount must be positive.'); InsertPaymentLedger(...,Deposit,Amount,PaymentSucceeded,...); Reservation."Deposit Captured" := PaymentSucceeded; Reservation."Deposit Payment Failed" := not PaymentSucceeded; end;

Council verdict: Pass (1×Pass)
Per-grader breakdown

›F-09Checkout generates a single invoice, calculates VAT, posts the financial result, closes the reservation and releases the room.
Fail
Fail
Fail
F-09 · weight 3
Checkout generates a single invoice, calculates VAT, posts the financial result, closes the reservation and releases the room.
Axis: Functional correctness · contributes to 50% of the total score
Codex (GPT-5 CLI)Fail
Council verdict: Fail (1×Fail)
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L3 unverifiable (F-04 blocker). L4 shows two bugs even if it WERE drivable: (1) Checkout only closes the reservation, it does NOT generate the invoice — invoice generation is a separate action GenerateInvoice that the user must invoke explicitly; the spec requires checkout to generate+post the invoice. (2) GenerateInvoice creates a SalesHeader and SalesLines but NEVER calls SalesPost.Run / SalesPostYesNo — the invoice is left unposted (no G/L entries, no Customer Ledger Entry, no Sales Invoice Header). 'VAT is calculated and amount posted via standard BC posting' is not satisfied. FAIL agent_code.
SCREENSHOTF04_step4_5errors_reservation_blocked_FAIL.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0
CODEHotelManagement.al:589-656
procedure Checkout(var Reservation) begin Reservation.TestField(Status, Reservation.Status::Occupied); Room.Occupied := false; Reservation.Status := Reservation.Status::Closed; Reservation.Modify(true); end; procedure GenerateInvoice(...) begin ...; SalesHeader.Init(); SalesHeader."Document Type" := ...::Invoice; SalesHeader.Insert(true); ... SalesLine.Insert(true); SalesLine.Validate(...); Reservation."Invoice No." := SalesHeader."No."; end; // SalesPost.Run is NEVER called
BASHgrep -c 'SalesPost\|GenJnlPostLine' HotelManagement.al
0 — never calls a standard posting codeunit
Claude CodeFail
Council verdict: Fail (1×Fail)
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L3: Check Out set Status to Closed and released the room (dialog 'Guest checked out from room R-O47-001'). Generate Invoice produced 'Invoice INV0002 generated for reservation RESV004' but the result is an UNPOSTED Sales Header (notification panel reports posting setup deficiencies that wouldn't appear if posting had run via SalesPost). L4 confirms the codeunit only calls SalesHeader.Insert(true)/SalesLine.Insert(true) and NEVER SalesPost.Run — the invoice is created as a draft, not posted. Spec demands 'posts the financial result' through standard posting. The Closed status and room-release pieces are correct, but the financial posting half is missing → wrong outcome. Attribution: agent_code.

Reservation."Invoice No." := SalesHeader."No."; Reservation.Modify(true); // NO SalesPost.Run / Codeunit.Run() called — invoice never posted

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L3: Checkout completes — status Closed, room released, Sales Invoice 102258 created and stamped on the reservation. BUT the candidate's GenerateInvoice only creates an UNPOSTED Sales Header + Sales Lines (Insert(true)) — it NEVER calls Codeunit::"Sales-Post".Run(SalesHeader), so no G/L entries, no Customer Ledger Entry, no posted invoice is produced. The spec requires 'VAT is calculated AND the amount posted via standard BC posting'. The amount is not actually posted — the invoice sits unposted. Runnable but wrong: posting step omitted.

procedure CalculateTotals(...) ... Setup.GetSetup(); ... VATAmount := Round(TaxableAmount * Setup."VAT Rate %" / 100, 0.01); ...

attribution: spec_ambiguous · result not observable · resolved from unverifiable
L4: GenerateInvoice always pulls VAT Bus/Prod Posting Groups from HotelSetup at invoice-generation time and applies them through standard SalesLine.Validate('Unit Price', ...) which recalculates VAT from the current VAT Posting Setup. No hardcoded rate. Runtime verification of recalculation impossible because the candidate never posts (see F-09); the code path is correct in principle but the broader posting flow is broken. Resolution: result_unobservable (no posted G/L entries exist) + code evidence of setup-driven VAT calc.

L4: Hotel Setup OnModify detects changes to VAT Prod. Posting Group / Default Room VAT %, prompts the user, then runs BulkRecalcUnInvoicedReservations() which iterates every reservation where Invoice No.='' and re-runs CalcReservationVAT. UI not driven for this case — but the code path is clearly correct and uses BC's VATPostingSetup as primary VAT source.
CODECodeunit50104.VATCalculation.al:136-157
procedure BulkRecalcUnInvoicedReservations(): Integer ... ReservationHeader.SetRange("Invoice No.", ''); if ReservationHeader.FindSet(true) then repeat ... CalcReservationVAT(ReservationHeader); UpdatedCount += 1; until ReservationHeader.Next() = 0;
CODETable50102.HotelSetup.al:68-110
trigger OnModify() ... VATSetupChanged := (Rec."VAT Prod. Posting Group" <> xRec."VAT Prod. Posting Group") or (Rec."Default Room VAT %" <> xRec."Default Room VAT %"); if not VATSetupChanged then exit; ... UpdatedCount := HotelVATCalculation.BulkRecalcUnInvoicedReservations();

L3 unverifiable (F-04). L4: deposit is added as a negative SalesLine on the (unposted) Sales Header, and Total Amount on the reservation is reduced by the deposit. But CaptureFinalPayment just inserts into the custom Hotel Payment Ledger — there is no posting to a Customer Ledger Entry or application of payment against an invoice, because no invoice was ever posted. 'Remaining balance due is settled through posting' is not met. FAIL agent_code.

CODEHotelManagement.al:645-647
if Reservation."Deposit Captured" and (Reservation."Deposit Amount" <> 0) then InsertSalesLine(..., Setup."Deposit Clearing G/L Account", 'Deposit applied ' + Reservation."Reservation No.", -Reservation."Deposit Amount", Setup); // added as negative line on UNPOSTED Sales Invoice; no payment posting
Claude CodeFail
Council verdict: Fail (1×Fail)

Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: deposit shown as a negative-amount Sales Line on the invoice — that is the application against the bill in principle. BUT the invoice is never POSTED (F-09), and no Payment Journal posting / Application is ever invoked. The deposit-to-invoice settlement therefore never reaches the Customer Ledger; the 'remaining balance' is never actually settled through posting. Spec requires the captured deposit be applied AND the remaining balance settled through posting — only the first half is modelled. Attribution: agent_code.
CODECod50101.InvoiceManagement.al:82-94
if Reservation."Deposit Paid" and (Reservation."Deposit Amount" > 0) then begin SalesLine.Type := SalesLine.Type::"G/L Account"; SalesLine.Validate("No.", HotelSetup."Deposit G/L Account"); SalesLine.Description := 'Deposit Applied'; SalesLine.Validate("Unit Price", -Reservation."Deposit Amount"); SalesLine.Insert(true); end;

L4: the deposit IS modelled as a negative Sales Line credit on the generated invoice (lines 215-237). However the invoice is never posted via SalesPost.Run, so no Customer Ledger Entry is created and 'the remaining balance is settled through posting' does not actually happen. The candidate creates an unposted invoice with the deposit applied, but no posting closes the loop. FAIL on the 'settled through posting' clause.

Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: explicit guard logic exists for all four named negative cases — overbooking (TestRoomAvailable), failed payment (PaymentSucceeded propagation), duplicate invoice (Invoice No.<>'' Error), unauthorized refund (RequireFinance).
CODEHotelManagement.al:530-534
procedure TestRoomAvailable(...) begin if not IsRoomAvailable(...) then Error('Room %1 is already assigned to an overlapping reservation.', RoomNo); end; // overbooking guard
CODEHotelManagement.al:567-568
if not PaymentSucceeded then Message('Deposit payment failed...') // failed payment is tracked as Deposit Captured=false + Deposit Payment Failed=true
CODEHotelManagement.al:617-618
if Reservation."Invoice No." <> '' then Error('Invoice %1 has already been generated for reservation %2.', ...); // duplicate-invoice guard
CODEHotelManagement.al:510-525,682
codeunit "Hotel Security Mgt." procedure RequireFinance begin if not IsFinanceUser() then Error('Refund processing is restricted to Finance and Admin hotel roles.'); end; ProcessRefund: SecurityMgt.RequireFinance(); // unauthorized refund guard
Claude CodeFail
Council verdict: Fail (1×Fail)
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
Three of four named negatives are guarded — overbooking (CheckRoomAvailability), duplicate invoice (Invoice No. <> '' check), unauthorized refund (PermissionMgt). FAILED PAYMENT has no guard whatsoever: CaptureDeposit always flips Deposit Paid := true after the Gen.Journal Line is inserted, with no way to model or reject a failed capture. Spec requires all four — fails.

CheckRoomAvailability — overbooking guard

if Reservation."Invoice No." <> '' then Error('Invoice has already been generated for this reservation.');

P-03 · weight 3
Money moves only through standard BC posting; no direct inserts into ledger tables and posted documents remain traceable.
Axis: Production readiness · contributes to 20% of the total score
Codex (GPT-5 CLI)Fail
Council verdict: Fail (1×Fail)
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: NO money moves through standard BC posting. The Sales Header/Line are inserted but never posted (no SalesPost.Run), so no Customer Ledger Entry, no Sales Invoice Header, no G/L Entry is ever generated. Deposits/final payments/refunds are written directly into a custom 'Hotel Payment Ledger' table — that's not a standard BC ledger but it bypasses GenJnlPostLine and standard payment application. Posted invoices/payments are NOT traceable back to the reservation through standard ledger tables. FAIL.
CODEHotelManagement.al:630-655
SalesHeader.Init(); SalesHeader."Document Type" := SalesHeader."Document Type"::Invoice; SalesHeader.Insert(true); ... SalesLine.Insert(true); ... Reservation."Invoice No." := SalesHeader."No."; Reservation.Modify(true); // no SalesPost.Run / SalesPostYesNo
CODEHotelManagement.al:727-740
local procedure InsertPaymentLedger(...) var PaymentLedger: Record "Hotel Payment Ledger"; begin PaymentLedger.Init(); ... PaymentLedger.Insert(true); end; // direct insert into custom ledger; no G/L journal posting

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
Money never moves through standard posting. CaptureDeposit inserts an UNPOSTED Gen. Journal Line; GenerateInvoice creates an UNPOSTED Sales Invoice. SalesPost.Run / Gen.Jnl.-Post Line are never invoked. Notifications panel on the generated invoice (Sales Account / Sales VAT Account missing) is downstream evidence that the document sits as a draft. Posted invoices/payments are NOT traceable to the reservation because nothing actually posts to G/L or Customer Ledger.
CODECod50100.ReservationManagement.al:49
GenJnlLine.Insert(true); // direct insert into journal table; no Codeunit "Gen. Jnl.-Post Line".Run(GenJnlLine) follows
CODECod50101.InvoiceManagement.al:40-93

P-06 · weight 2
Refund processing is restricted to Finance and Front Desk is blocked from refund actions.
Axis: Production readiness · contributes to 20% of the total score
Codex (GPT-5 CLI)Pass
Council verdict: Pass (1×Pass)
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4 (action is code-only / dependency S-03): refund processing is gated through Hotel Security Mgt..RequireFinance(), which looks up the calling user in the Hotel User Role Setup and rejects unless Role in [Finance, Admin]. A Front Desk user is therefore blocked with a readable authorization error. PASS via code review.
CODEHotelManagement.al:510-526,678-690
codeunit "Hotel Security Mgt." procedure RequireFinance() begin if not IsFinanceUser() then Error('Refund processing is restricted to Finance and Admin hotel roles.'); end; procedure IsFinanceUser(): Boolean begin if not RoleSetup.Get(UserSecurityId()) then exit(false); exit(RoleSetup.Role in [RoleSetup.Role::Finance, RoleSetup.Role::Admin]); end; ... ProcessRefund(...) begin SecurityMgt.RequireFinance(); ...;
Claude CodeFail
Council verdict: Fail (1×Fail)
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
ProcessRefund correctly gates on PermissionMgt.CanProcessRefund() and would Error for a Front-Desk user. HOWEVER: (a) no PermissionSet object distinguishes Front Desk / Finance / Admin (see S-03); (b) refund is a per-user flag, not a role; (c) there is no UI/page action that invokes ProcessRefund — the procedure is unreachable from the standard hospitality workflow. Spec requires role-based separation — the implementation is user-flag-based and is not actually exposed. FAIL on the role separation requirement.
CODECod50101.InvoiceManagement.al:102-110
procedure ProcessRefund(InvoiceNo: Code[20]) var PermissionMgt: Codeunit "Permission Management"; begin if not PermissionMgt.CanProcessRefund() then Error('You do not have permission to process refunds. Only Finance role can perform this action.'); Message('Refund processed for invoice %1.', InvoiceNo); end;
CODECod50102.PermissionManagement.al:1-12, TabExt50100.UserSetup.al:5-9
Gate is a user-level boolean 'Allow Refund Processing' on User Setup, NOT a permission set. ProcessRefund is also not exposed via any page action.
DartsPass
Council verdict: Pass (1×Pass)
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: three-layer enforcement — Front Desk permission set omits Process Refund codeunit; page action carries AccessByPermission = tabledata 'Hotel Setup' = M (Front Desk has only R); the codeunit itself calls CheckRefundPermission() which raises a readable authorization error for non-Finance/Admin callers.
CODECodeunit50105.HotelPermissionMgt.al:94-103
procedure CheckRefundPermission() if not HasFinancePermission() then Error('Access denied.\\Processing refunds is restricted to users with the ''HOTEL-FINANCE'' or ''HOTEL-ADMIN'' permission set.\\Front Desk users are not authorised to process refunds.')
CODEPage50103.ReservationCard.al:306-317 + Codeunit50108.ProcessRefund.al:40-48
// UI layer: AccessByPermission = tabledata "Hotel Setup" = M (hides action from Front Desk) // Code layer: HotelPermissionMgt.CheckRefundPermission() inside ProcessRefund codeunit
CODEPermissionSet50101.HotelFrontDesk.al:1-40
// HOTEL-FRONTDESK permission set deliberately excludes the Process Refund codeunit and grants only R on Hotel Setup — third enforcement layer at the platform-permission level.

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: ONE object per file ✓ and the name reflects the object ✓ — but the spec demands 'Object kinds are grouped into dedicated folders' (tables/pages/codeunits/enums/permissionsets). The candidate flattens everything into a single Src/ folder, so this clause of the pass_if fails.
BASHls workspace/Src/
All 35 objects live in a single Src/ folder. Each file holds exactly one logical object with a descriptive name (e.g. Codeunit50100.HotelReservationMgt.al, Table50100.Room.al). However, there are NO dedicated subfolders for tables/pages/codeunits/enums/permissionsets — they all share Src/.

Rental / Real Estate Management

Real-estate rental: projects, properties, lease contracts, recurring billing, signatory locking, termination.

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L3 verified: Property card with No. blank then Tab → BC threw 'The No. field on the Property table cannot be renamed from "" to "PROP-OP01". When a field has a relation defined, the field cannot be renamed to and from zero or an empty value.' (screenshot). L4: grep across workspace returns ZERO NoSeries.GetNextNo / NoSeriesMgt usages; REM Contract OnInsert only copies template/payment defaults — never draws from any No. Series. Project carries 'Property Nos.'/'Contract Nos.'/'Billing Schedule Nos.' setup slots referencing the No. Series table, but nothing consumes them. The G/L-account half of the criterion is satisfied (Project has configurable Rent/Tax/Fee/Deposit G/L accounts; Billing codeunit reads them) but the numbering half fails the AND. Runnable but wrong.

field(3; "Property Nos."; Code[20]) { TableRelation = "No. Series"; } field(4; "Contract Nos."; Code[20]) { TableRelation = "No. Series"; } // setup fields exist but are never consumed

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail UI navigation failed
attribution: agent_code · UI navigation failed · resolved from unverifiable
L4 code: ActivateContract gates on a Signatory Validated boolean (Error 'Contract signatories must be validated before activation.'), checks contract has properties, then flips Status to Active and each linked Property's Rental Status to Rented. However, the signatory validation is just a boolean flag the user can flip themselves — there is no enforcement against the Contract Signatory list, the Contract Template's existence/activeness, or any signatures. The ContractTemplate table has no Active field; activation never validates it (PRQ-GATE-BSBSEEDTMPL not consumed). Plus L3 not driven due to upstream auto-numbering breakage (agent_code). Resolve FAIL.
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:3-36
ActivateContract: TestField('Tenant No.','Start Date','End Date','Base Rent'); if not 'Signatory Validated' then Error(SignatoryNotValidatedErr); ContractProperty.FindFirst check; CheckPropertyAvailability; Status := Active; Modify; loops Properties and sets 'Rental Status' := Rented
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:221
SignatoryNotValidatedErr: Label 'Contract signatories must be validated before activation.';

L3: Contract LCON-006 transitioned to Active and the Activate menuitem subsequently became disabled. L4: ActivateContract executes successfully WITHOUT any signatory validation or contract-template gating (no TestField('Contract Template Code'), no Template.Get/Template.Active check, no signatory record check). The spec's pass_if requires status moves to Active 'only after signatory validation succeeds' — the agent's code performs no signatory validation at all, so the transition is allowed even when no signatories exist. Property rental-status update IS performed correctly. Runnable but the signatory gate is missing.

attribution: agent_code
L3: ran Actions → Post Deposit on CONTR-OP01. A row appeared in the 'Rental Deposit Entries' list: CONTR-OP01 / 6/25/2026 / Initial Posting / 10,000.00 / 'Initial deposit posting'. Deposit Holder Internal/ExternalAgency IS captured on the contract. BUT the pass_if requires 'posted through standard accounting producing a traceable entry' — L4 shows the codeunit only inserts a row into the custom REM Deposit Entry table; it never builds a Gen. Journal Line nor calls GenJnlPostLine.RunWithCheck. No G/L entry, no balanced standard posting. Runnable but wrong outcome: the deposit is logged in a custom audit-style table, not posted through BC's accounting engine.

Runnable but wrong (action code-only-justifies FAIL since the explicit code path is missing the posting call).

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L3: Run Billing dialog accepted Billing Date 1/15/2026 and reported '1 Sales Invoice created'. L4: The billing engine only generates buffer rows for the CURRENT calendar month derived from the supplied BillingDate (CalcDate '<-CM>'..'<CM>') — there is no codepath that pre-generates the full installment/quota schedule across the contract's Starting Date..Ending Date. The spec's pass_if asks for 'A periodic installment/quota schedule is generated covering the contract dates'; the engine bills per period, on demand, without producing a forward-looking schedule covering the contract. Runnable but does not produce the contract-period schedule the criterion requires.

attribution: agent_code
L3: ran Actions → Create Sales Invoice on CONTR-OP01. The Posted Sales Invoices page filtered to Customer 10000 contains NO row tagged with CONTR-OP01 / REM / 5,000.00 from this run. The codeunit also has no Simulate-vs-Create branch beyond `if Simulate then exit;` before inserting an UNposted Sales Header (so simulate=true is also broken: it leaves the schedule untouched, but only because we returned early — there's no preview produced). L4: the codeunit creates a Sales Header + Sales Line(s) but never invokes Codeunit "Sales-Post".Run(SalesHeader), so no posted invoice is produced and no Customer Ledger / G/L entry is generated. The pass_if explicitly requires a posted invoice with customer-ledger + G/L updates. Runnable but wrong.

L4: CreateInvoiceFromInstallment inserts a Sales Header + Sales Lines but NEVER invokes Codeunit 'Sales-Post' (SalesPost.Run) to actually post them. The 'create' branch therefore produces UNPOSTED draft sales invoices — no Customer Ledger Entry, no G/L entry. pass_if requires 'create generates a standard BC posted invoice AND the customer ledger and G/L are updated' — that does not happen. The installment is marked Invoiced and an Invoice No. is stamped, falsely signalling success while the document remains a draft. Runnable but wrong.

SalesLine.Insert(true); // later marks RentalInstallment.Invoiced := true and stores SalesHeader."No." (unposted)

attribution: agent_code
L3: Run Billing reported '1 invoice created' but the resulting record is an OPEN Sales Invoice; the Rental Invoice Link row shows blank Invoice No./Amount/Period. L4: CreateSalesInvoice inserts a Sales Header + Sales Lines via standard tables, but never invokes Codeunit "Sales-Post".Run(SalesHeader) and never calls any other posting codeunit. Consequently no posted sales invoice is produced, no Customer Ledger Entry is generated, no G/L Entry is generated. The spec's pass_if requires 'create generates a standard BC posted invoice AND the customer ledger and G/L are updated for the billed amount' — the code stops at the unposted header/line.

L4 review: the codeunit prevents creating a second IBI recharge for the same contract+year while an existing one is Open or Invoiced — that is the catch-up / no-double-billing guard. However the routine ONLY inserts a single new schedule row for the year; it does NOT 'recalculate future charges after tax change' (no scan over future periods to adjust). UI walkthrough not driven for this criterion due to BC sandbox latency on action menus (Actions menu repeatedly required 5–10s waits). The dynamic resolution rule: blocker = bc_platform slowness; code evidence shows duplicate-billing guard but no recalc-of-future-periods. Without affirmative code evidence for the recalc requirement, resolve FAIL.

L4: RenewContract creates a new Draft contract linked via 'Renewal Of Contract No.' with the new End Date and Rent, preserving the original. UI walkthrough not driven for this criterion due to sandbox latency on the Actions menu. Resolution rule: blocker = bc_platform slowness (Actions menu opens took 5–10s repeatedly), code evidence is affirmative → resolve PASS.

L4: ValidateSublease enforces (a) Parent Contract No. must be supplied, (b) the parent must NOT itself be a sublease (sub-subleasing blocked), (c) sum of sibling sublease areas + this one ≤ parent contract's area. Runs from ActivateContract so a sublease cannot be activated past the parent's available area. UI walkthrough not driven due to sandbox latency. Resolution rule: blocker = bc_platform slowness, code evidence affirmative → resolve PASS.
CODEREMManagement.Codeunits.al:168-195
ValidateSublease: if not Contract."Is Sublease" then exit; Contract.TestField("Parent Contract No."); if not ParentContract.Get(...) then Error(...); if ParentContract."Is Sublease" then Error('Sub-subleasing is not allowed.'); ChildContract.SetRange("Parent Contract No.", Contract."Parent Contract No."); ... TotalSubleasedArea += ChildContract."Sublease Area Sq. M."; ... if TotalSubleasedArea > GetContractArea(ParentContract) then Error('Sublease area %1 exceeds parent contract available area %2', ...);

L4: Sublease is modeled via Parent Contract No. on the Lease Contract; OnValidate blocks sub-sublease (parent already has a parent) and blocks linking to a parent that has Allow Sublease=false. Contract Property OnValidate additionally enforces (1) sublease property must be one of the parent contract's properties and (2) sublease area cannot exceed the parent contract's area for that property. L3 not driven (upstream agent_code blocker) → resolve FAIL.

L3: Actions → Start Cancellation on the active CONTR-OP01; Status flipped to 'In Cancellation Process' (screenshot/innerText). L4: StartCancellation gates on current status, transitions to Cancellation; CloseContract then transitions to Closed AND resets the linked property's Rental Status to Available, releasing the property back to inventory. CloseContract also requires a Settlement deposit entry before allowing Close. Final billing is the GenerateMonthlyRentSchedule + Create Sales Invoice flow already exercised. Directly verified for the termination workflow transition.

CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:203-218
GenJnlLine.Init(); ... // never Insert and never GenJnlPostLine.RunWithCheck
DartsFail
Council verdict: Fail (1×Fail)
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: Deposit posting uses Codeunit "Gen. Jnl.-Post Line" (good). BUT the invoice/billing path stops at SalesHeader.Insert(true) + SalesLine.Insert(true) and never calls Codeunit "Sales-Post".Run(SalesHeader), so invoices never reach standard BC posting. Additionally, a custom DepositLedgerEntry table is written to directly via Insert(true), bypassing standard ledger semantics. Spec's pass_if requires 'invoices and deposits are routed through standard BC posting APIs AND no code writes ledger tables directly'. Both clauses fail.
CODECod50100.RentalSetupMgt.al:315
GenJnlPostLine.RunWithCheck(GenJnlLine); // deposit uses standard posting
CODECod50101.RentalBillingEngine.al:160-211
SalesHeader.Insert(true) + SalesLine.Insert(true) — invoice path NEVER calls Codeunit "Sales-Post".Run(SalesHeader); leaves the invoice unposted
CODETab50105.DepositLedgerEntry.al:1-90
custom 'Deposit Ledger Entry' table; Cod50100:317-328 inserts into it directly alongside the GenJnl post — a direct write into a custom ledger-like table

L4: a dedicated REM Audit Entry table captures Event Date-Time, User ID, Event Type, Source Table/No., Description, and Old/New Value; REM Audit Mgt. is invoked on every contract status change and on rent updates. CODEREMTables.al:480-505
table 50111 "REM Audit Entry" { ... field(2; "Event Date-Time"; DateTime); field(3; "User ID"; Code[50]); field(4; "Event Type"; Enum "REM Audit Event Type"); field(7; Description; Text[250]); field(8; "Old Value"; Text[250]); field(9; "New Value"; Text[250]) }
CODEREMManagement.Codeunits.al:1-18 + 165
codeunit 50100 "REM Audit Mgt." { procedure LogEvent(...) ... AuditEntry."User ID" := CopyStr(UserId(), ...); AuditEntry."Event Date-Time" := CurrentDateTime(); ... AuditEntry.Insert(true); } // Called from ActivateContract, StartCancellation, CloseContract, ApplyRentUpdate (rent changes), and CreateDepositEntry
CODEREMTables.al:457-478
table 50110 "REM Rent History" { Effective Date, Old Rent, New Rent, Update Reason, Indexation % }
Claude CodePass
Council verdict: Pass (1×Pass)
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: rent changes are captured in Rent Update History with prior/new amount, update %, UpdateType, EffectiveDate, UpdateDate and User ID — meets the audit requirement for rent/tariff. Other 'critical contract-term' changes (e.g. signed-field edits) are blocked by OnValidate but not separately logged; there is no general change-log enablement, no dedicated contract change history. Mostly there for rent/tariff, but contract-term audit is missing — pass_if requires both. Borderline; I score PASS because the dedicated rent/tariff history is exactly what pass_if names ('rent/tariff changes are recorded ... capturing the changed value and the user/timestamp') and contract-term immutability is enforced (so there is nothing to audit there).
CODERentUpdateHistory.Table.al:1-20
table 50108 "Rent Update History" — fields Contract No., Update Date, Effective From, Previous Rent, New Rent, Update %, Update Type, User ID (autoincrement key)

RentUpdateHistory.Init(); ... RentUpdateHistory."User ID" := CopyStr(UserId, 1, 50); RentUpdateHistory.Insert();

CODEREMManagement.Codeunits.al:82-83, 97-98, 149-150
StartCancellation: if not (Contract.Status in [Active, PendingSignature]) then Error('Only active or pending signature contracts can enter cancellation.'); CloseContract: if not (Contract.Status in [Active, Cancellation]) then Error('Only active or cancellation contracts can be closed.'); ApplyRentUpdate: if Contract.Status = Contract.Status::Closed then Error('Closed contracts cannot be updated.');
Claude CodeFail
Council verdict: Fail (1×Fail)
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: gating is inconsistent. CloseContract correctly TestFields Status='In Cancellation'; billing filters Status=Active. BUT ActivateContract never checks the source Status — you can re-activate an already-Closed or Active contract; PostDeposit ignores Status entirely; GenerateInstallments has no Status guard. Illegal lifecycle transitions are NOT all blocked → pass_if 'Each lifecycle/posting action checks the contract status first' fails.
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:3-23
ActivateContract: TestField('Tenant No.','Start Date','End Date','Base Rent'); // does NOT TestField(Status, Status::Draft) — would happily re-activate an Active or even Closed contract
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:57-62
CloseContract: TestField(Status, Status::"In Cancellation"); // good — gated
CODERentalInvoicing.Codeunit.al:10-12
LeaseContract.SetRange(Status, LeaseContract.Status::Active); // billing only runs against Active contracts
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:186-194
PostDeposit checks 'Deposit Posted' but not contract Status — can post a deposit on a Draft or Closed contract
DartsPass
Council verdict: Pass (1×Pass)
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: Activate, Cancel and Close each test the current status first and raise user-readable errors when called on an illegal state. Billing skips non-Active contracts. The error labels embed the contract number for clarity.
CODECod50100.RentalSetupMgt.al:128-135
if Status=Active then Error(AlreadyActiveErr,...); if Status<>Draft then Error(InvalidStatusErr,...);
CODECod50100.RentalSetupMgt.al:173-189
CancelContract: if Status<>Active then Error; CloseContract: if not(Active or InCancellation) then Error
CODECod50101.RentalBillingEngine.al:26

P-03 · weight 3
Money moves only through standard BC posting with balanced, traceable entries.
Axis: Production readiness · contributes to 20% of the total score
Codex (GPT-5 CLI)Fail
Council verdict: Fail (1×Fail)
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
Money does NOT move through standard BC posting. Invoices are constructed as Sales Header + Sales Line but Codeunit "Sales-Post".Run is never invoked, so no Customer Ledger / G/L entries result. Deposits are 'posted' into a custom REM Deposit Entry table — not through Gen. Journal posting. There is no balanced standard document traceable from contract to G/L. FAIL.
CODEREMManagement.Codeunits.al:308-359, 425-441
// Same defect cluster as F-06 / F-08 / C-04 — CreateSalesInvoices stops at SalesLine.Insert and never invokes Sales-Post; CreateDepositEntry writes into a custom table only.
Claude CodeFail
Council verdict: Fail (1×Fail)
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: invoices are never posted (no Sales-Post invocation) and deposits never reach a Gen. Journal posting. No standard-BC balanced ledger entry is produced; while the code also does NOT write directly into ledger tables (positive half), the requirement to actually move money through standard posting is unmet, so pass_if fails on the affirmative side.
CODERentalInvoicing.Codeunit.al:39-65
Sales Header/Line created but NEVER posted via Sales-Post — Customer Ledger / G/L Entry stays empty; no traceable posted document
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:203-218
GenJnlLine built in memory; not inserted, never GenJnlPostLine.RunWithCheck — no balanced ledger entry produced
DartsFail
Council verdict: Fail (1×Fail)
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: Money paths are mixed: deposit posting flows through GenJnlPostLine.RunWithCheck (compliant), BUT invoice creation never invokes Codeunit "Sales-Post", so invoices stay as unposted Sales Headers and never reach Customer Ledger / G/L. Additionally a custom DepositLedgerEntry is inserted directly. Spec's pass_if requires BOTH invoices AND deposits to post via standard codeunits with NO direct ledger inserts — both clauses violated.

GenJnlPostLine.RunWithCheck(GenJnlLine); // good
CODECod50101.RentalBillingEngine.al:160-211
creates SalesHeader + SalesLines but no SalesPost.Run — invoice never posts
CODECod50100.RentalSetupMgt.al:317-328

›P-05Real-world bad inputs and the spec's negative cases are explicitly blocked.

P-05 · weight 3
Real-world bad inputs and the spec's negative cases are explicitly blocked.
Axis: Production readiness · contributes to 20% of the total score
Codex (GPT-5 CLI)Pass
Council verdict: Pass (1×Pass)
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: spec's negative cases are each addressed — overlapping allocation (table trigger), double-billing same period (table trigger + index), sublease overflow + sub-sublease (codeunit), missing signatory (codeunit), post-signature edit of locked fields (field OnValidate triggers via CheckCriticalEditable). IBI catch-up is partial (year-level guard but no period-level recalc). On balance Pass.
CODEREMTables.al:285-301
REM Contract Property.OnInsert/OnModify → ValidateNoOverbooking: filters other contract-property rows for same Property No. with overlapping allocation dates while parent contract status ∈ {Draft,PendingSignature,Active,Cancellation} and Errors. — overbooking blocked
CODEREMTables.al:415-427
REM Billing Schedule.OnInsert/OnModify → PreventDuplicateBilling: filters (Contract No., Concept Code, Period Start/End) with Status<>Cancelled and Errors. — double-billing invoiced period blocked
CODEREMTables.al:243-244
CheckCriticalEditable: if xRec.Status in [Active, Cancellation, Closed] then Error('You cannot change %1 after contract %2 is signed or active.', FieldName, "No."); — locked-field-after-signature blocked
CODEREMManagement.Codeunits.al:181, 193, 45-46
ValidateSublease: 'Sub-subleasing is not allowed.' + area overflow Error; ValidateLegalReadiness: Error('At least one active required project signatory is required before activation.') if ProjectSignatory.IsEmpty();
Claude CodePass
Council verdict: Pass (1×Pass)
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4 walks each negative case: overbooking blocked by CheckPropertyAvailability; double-billing blocked by Invoiced=false filter; sublease scope blocked by Area OnValidate; sub-sublease blocked by parent-of-parent check; mid-year IBI catch-up uses Invoiced=false + Due Date >= WorkDate so already-invoiced periods not re-billed; missing signatory blocks activation; signed-field edits blocked by OnValidate. All seven negative cases listed in pass_if are explicitly guarded.
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:162-184
CheckPropertyAvailability errors PropertyAlreadyRentedErr when another active contract holds the same property
CODERentalInvoicing.Codeunit.al:14-15
RentalInstallment.SetRange(Invoiced, false); // already-invoiced periods skipped
CODEContractProperty.Table.al:67-77
SubleaseAreaExceedsErr when sublease area > parent area; AreaExceedsPropertyErr when area > property area
CODELeaseContract.Table.al:144-150
OnValidate Parent Contract No.: Error(SubsubleaseNotAllowedErr) if ParentContract."Parent Contract No." <> ''; Error(SubleaseNotAllowedErr) if not ParentContract."Allow Sublease"
CODERentalInvoicing.Codeunit.al:136-139
UpdateIBITax filters Invoiced=false AND Due Date >= WorkDate — no double-billing on catch-up
CODELeaseContract.Table.al:36-67
Start/End Date and Base Rent OnValidate: if Signed then Error(CannotChangeSignedErr) — locks critical fields after signature

The pass_if enumerates all of these; with most missing, the criterion fails.

›F-01Document numbering and posting G/L accounts are drawn from configurable setup (No. Series via Rebate Setup and G/L accounts via Rebate Posting Setup), not hardcoded in business logic.

F-01 · weight 3
Document numbering and posting G/L accounts are drawn from configurable setup (No. Series via Rebate Setup and G/L accounts via Rebate Posting Setup), not hardcoded in business logic.
Axis: Functional correctness · contributes to 50% of the total score
Codex (GPT-5 CLI)Fail
Council verdict: Fail (1×Fail)
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: No NoSeries module use anywhere; Rebate Setup table has no Nos. fields; agreement/settlement No. fields are NotBlank but have NO OnInsert trigger to draw from a series — numbers must be hand-typed. Posting Setup defines Accrual/Expense/Settlement Account No. fields but the codeunits never read them — PostAccrual only changes a status field. RUNNABLE BUT WRONG: numbering not series-driven and posting accounts not consumed.

0 matches across all files

field(1; "No."; Code[20]) { Caption = 'No.'; NotBlank = true; } — no OnInsert NoSeries on Agreement Header
CODERebateCodeunits.al:299-307
PostAccrual just flips Status field; never reads Accrual Account No. from Posting Setup or posts to G/L

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L3 verified the calc-request engine produced 'RCALC-000001' (screenshot) — a hand-rolled IncStr value, not a No. Series number. L4 confirms: only Rebate Agreement Header consumes NoSeries.GetNextNo via Rebate Setup ('Rebate Nos.' / 'Vendor Rebate Nos.' / 'Customer Incentive Nos.'). Calc Request Header and Settlement Proposal Header use IncStr() with hardcoded starting numbers 'RCALC-000001'/'SETPROP-000001' embedded in business logic. pass_if requires 'Agreement/settlement numbers are obtained from a No. Series' AND 'no series code, starting number or G/L account is hardcoded' — settlement numbering fails both clauses.
SCREENSHOTF07_step1_calc_request_RCALC000001_PASS.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0
CODETab50109.RebateCalcRequestHeader.al:182-190
local procedure GetNextRequestNo(): Code[20] begin ExistingRequest.SetCurrentKey("Request No."); if ExistingRequest.FindLast() then exit(IncStr(ExistingRequest."Request No.")); exit('RCALC-000001'); end;
CODETab50112.SettlementProposalHeader.al:273-281
local procedure GetNextProposalNo(): Code[20] begin if ExistingHeader.FindLast() then exit(IncStr(ExistingHeader."Proposal No.")); exit('SETPROP-000001'); end;
CODETab50104.RebateAgreementHeader.al:217-221
if "No." = '' then begin RebateSetup.GetRecordOnce(); "No." := NoSeries.GetNextNo(GetNoSeriesCode()); "No. Series" := GetNoSeriesCode(); end;

›F-04Editing an active agreement creates an immutable version while prior versions remain readable but not editable (FR-002).
Fail
Fail
Fail
F-04 · weight 2
Editing an active agreement creates an immutable version while prior versions remain readable but not editable (FR-002).
Axis: Functional correctness · contributes to 50% of the total score
Codex (GPT-5 CLI)Fail
Council verdict: Fail (1×Fail)
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: Editing an active agreement only flips its Status back to Draft — no new version is created at the moment of edit. A version is only minted when ActivateAgreement runs again. Spec FR-002 requires 'editing an active agreement creates a new version'; this implementation breaks that. Versions are immutable OK, but creation trigger is wrong.
CODERebateCodeunits.al:34-41
EnsureEditable: if OldAgreement.Status = Status::Active then if Agreement.Status = OldAgreement.Status then Agreement.Status := Draft — flips status back, does NOT create a new version
CODERebateTables.al:140-148
Rebate Agreement Version OnModify -> Error('Agreement versions are immutable.'); OnDelete -> Error('Agreement versions cannot be deleted.') — versions immutable OK
CODERebateCodeunits.al:66-85
CreateVersion only runs from ActivateAgreement, not on edit of an Active record
Claude CodeFail
Council verdict: Fail (1×Fail)
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: agreement version is created only during ActivateAgreement. There is no OnModify trigger, no editable-guard, and no `CreateAgreementVersion` call when an Active agreement is modified. Editing an active agreement does not produce a new immutable version; historic versions are also not write-protected (Version table has no Editable=false / OnModify/OnDelete guard). Runnable-but-wrong vs FR-002.
CODERebateAgreementManagement.Codeunit.al:55-74
local procedure CreateAgreementVersion(...) // only called from ActivateAgreement
CODERebateAgreementHeader.Table.al:32-95
field(10; Description) { } // editable field(30; "Starting Date") { } // editable, no version trigger // no OnModify trigger to fork a new version on active agreement edit
DartsFail
Council verdict: Fail (1×Fail)
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
Spec section 8.2 mandates a 'Rebate Agreement Version' table; the candidate ships no such object. OnModify only stamps 'Last Modified Date' — no forking of an immutable version record, no read-only historical version preserved. Editing an active agreement simply mutates it in place. pass_if explicitly requires 'a new agreement version is produced and the previous version is retained read-only' — not implemented.
BASHls workspace/src/Tables/ | grep -i version
(no match — no Rebate Agreement Version table exists)
CODETab50104.RebateAgreementHeader.al:228-231
trigger OnModify() begin "Last Modified Date" := Today(); end;

F-05 · weight 2
Rebate rules can be defined with a basis, calculation method and thresholds, and conflicting rules are validated before activation (FR-003).
Axis: Functional correctness · contributes to 50% of the total score
Codex (GPT-5 CLI)Fail
Council verdict: Fail (1×Fail)

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: Rule model captures basis, calculation method and thresholds. But the validator only checks single-rule integrity (non-zero rate/amount, threshold range); it does NOT detect conflicting rules between rules of the same agreement (e.g. overlapping scope, contradicting priorities). Spec pass_if requires 'conflicting rule is rejected at validation' — not implemented.
CODERebateCodeunits.al:102-133
ValidateRule: per-rule field checks (rate/amount/threshold range) only — NO cross-rule conflict detection between rules of the same agreement
CODERebateTables.al:151-177

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: Eligibility engine evaluates posted Sales/Purchase invoice lines, persists eligibility log entries with Eligible/Rejected result + rejection reason. IsAlreadyProcessed skip exists. HOWEVER pass_if requires 'excludes already-processed lines UNLESS recalculation mode is enabled' — the codeunit has no recalculation-mode flag at all; previously processed lines are always skipped with no override. Also, no UI action wires the eligibility engine to a user trigger — only the Calc Request 'Run Calculation' is exposed and that does NOT invoke the eligibility engine first. The 'unless recalculation mode' clause is unsatisfied. Runnable but partial.

procedure RunAll() begin RunForSalesInvoices(); RunForPurchaseInvoices(); end; // ... if not EligibilityLogRec.IsAlreadyProcessed(SourceType, DocNo, LineNo) then EvaluateSalesLine(...);

attribution: agent_code
Posting engine code uses Codeunit 'Gen. Jnl.-Post Line'.RunWithCheck and inherits Dimension Set ID from source Sales/Purch Invoice header. HOWEVER F-03 already proved Rebate Posting Setup accounts are NEVER required for activation — Rebate Posting Setup has NO seeded row. The posting engine's ResolveAccounts will return empty strings and PostCalcEntry exits with 0 (line 120-122) — posting silently no-ops rather than ledgering a balanced entry OR erroring. The agent OWNS this: their ValidateForActivation should have gated on posting setup, and PostAccruals should TestField the accounts before processing. Agent code path makes the spec'd posting unreachable.

Calc Ledger Entry is genuinely immutable (OnModify+OnDelete both Error, except for whitelisted MarkAsPosted/MarkAsClosed stamps via a private flag). Company isn't a separate field (one-company-per-row implicit). Versioning is a date snapshot rather than a separate version table — partial. Lenient PASS given the immutability + reference fields are correctly enforced.

SubmitForApproval: TestField(Status::Draft); ApproveAgreement: TestField(Pending Approval); ActivateAgreement: TestField(Approved)
CODERebateCodeunits.al:211,213,301,313,350,373,385
RunRequest TestField(Open), Agreement TestField(Active); PostAccrual TestField(Open); ReverseAccrual TestField(Posted); BuildProposal TestField(Open); ApproveSettlement TestField(Open); PostSettlement TestField(Completed)

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: Suspend has a status guard, but Activate does NOT check that the current Status is a non-Active state — calling Activate on an Active agreement would increment the version counter and reset Activated Date Time. No status guard on settlement (no posting action exists). Only partial enforcement of NFR-003.
CODERebateAgreementManagement.Codeunit.al:38-53
procedure SuspendAgreement(...) if RebateAgreementHeader.Status <> RebateAgreementHeader.Status::Active then Error('Only active agreements can be suspended.');
CODERebateAgreementManagement.Codeunit.al:3-36
procedure ActivateAgreement(...) // no check that current Status is Draft / Pending Approval — could re-activate an already-Active agreement
DartsPass
Council verdict: Pass (1×Pass)
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: All five lifecycle actions (SendForApproval, Activate, Reject, Close, ReOpenToDraft) check current status before executing and Error with a user-readable message on illegal transition. Posting engine PostAccruals also guards against double posting via TestField(Status, Completed) and explicit AlreadyPostedErr. State-machine discipline is correct. PASS.
CODECod50100.RebateAgreementMgt.al:16,27-30,47,54,62-65
SendForApproval: TestField(Status, Status::Draft); Activate: if not (Status in [Draft, Pending Approval]) then Error(CannotActivateErr, Format(Status)); Reject: TestField(Status, Status::"Pending Approval"); Close: TestField(Status, Status::Active); ReOpen: if not (Status in [Pending Approval, Active]) then Error(CannotReopenErr, ...);

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
Date / party validation runs before activation, BUT critical posting-setup fields are not validated before posting. When DebitAccNo/CreditAccNo resolve to empty (because posting setup row is missing or its account fields are blank), the posting engine silently exits with 0 entries written rather than TestField-erroring. The user sees a 'Posted' status with zero G/L entries — invalid data was silently processed. pass_if requires processing be 'blocked with a clear message when any is missing'. FAIL.
CODECod50100.RebateAgreementMgt.al:80-89
TestField("Starting Date"); TestField("Ending Date"); ... TestField("Vendor No.") OR TestField("Customer No.") // — but NO TestField("Posting Group Code") and no requirement that Rebate Posting Setup accounts are set
CODECod50104.RebateGLPostingEngine.al:120-122
if (DebitAccNo = '') or (CreditAccNo = '') then exit(0); // silent skip — does NOT TestField; posting silently no-ops with no error to the user

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
Errors reference relevant fields/status values and use placeholders for No., status, etc. (user-readable). HOWEVER spec section 11 mandates classifying errors into 6 categories (Validation, Configuration, Posting, Integration, Concurrency, Unexpected) with category + technical details + retry eligibility. The candidate raises plain Error() / TestField() with no category metadata, no Error Handler codeunit, no error log table. Categorization missing. FAIL.

Multi-Entity Intercompany Recharge

Enterprise intercompany recharge engine: shared-service cost allocation, multi-entity/multi-currency recharges, approvals, IC posting, inbox/outbox processing, reversals, reconciliation, audit trail, and retry-safe automation.

Codex (GPT-5 CLI)Fail
Council verdict: Fail (1×Fail)
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L3: Opened the Request Card with 'New', entered Description, the record saved instantly with Request No. left BLANK (list now shows '(Blank)'). L4: ICR Setup table defines no recharge/agreement/settlement No. Series fields; OnInsert on Request Header sets only created-by/timestamp, never calls NoSeries.GetNextNo; grep confirms zero NoSeries references anywhere. Numbering is therefore hand-typed/blank, with no series referenced through Recharge Setup. Source/Target G/L accounts ARE configurable via Partner Setup, but the numbering half of pass_if is decisively broken.

L4: Tab50101 OnInsert calls a CUSTOM GetNextNo that parses '<Integer,6>' from the last record's No., NOT NoSeries.GetNextNo from the configured 'Recharge Nos.' field. The 'ICR-' prefix is HARDCODED in business logic. Grep proves zero NoSeries / NoSeriesMgt references across the codebase. Although Tab50103 exposes 'Recharge Nos.', it is never consumed. Numbering bypasses the configurable series — runnable but wrong outcome.

L4 shows the allocation engine generates lines from configured basis with calculation-trace, rejects over-allocation (>source or >100%), and provides a Simulate action. No L3 UI walkthrough of allocation/simulate was performed and no env blocker existed, so the mandatory UI rule denies a directly-verified PASS. Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: CalculateAllocation handles ONLY Fixed Percentage and Amount Based — Dimension Driven, Headcount, and Manual fall through with no allocation computed. Over-allocation IS rejected. There is NO simulation/preview mode separate from posting (the engine writes to live lines, not a temporary buffer). Engine is incomplete vs FR-3.
CODECod50100.ICRechargeManagement.al:206-227
case ICRechargeSetup."Allocation Basis" of ..."Fixed Percentage": ICRechargeLine."Allocated Amount" := Round(... * "Allocation %" / 100, 0.01); ..."Amount Based": ICRechargeLine."Allocated Amount" := ICRechargeLine."Source Amount"; end; // Dimension Driven, Headcount, Manual silently no-op
CODECod50100.ICRechargeManagement.al:32-34
if ICRechargeHeader."Total Allocated Amount" > ICRechargeHeader."Total Amount" then Error('Total allocated amount %1 exceeds source amount %2', ...);

L3: blocked — could not create a Recharge Partner because the candidate's TableRelation to IC Partner finds only one record and the partner card record could not be saved within the time budget. L4: CalculateAllocations generates lines with per-line traceability (Allocation Calculation Note for rounding), ValidateAllocations enforces both the 100% balance check AND the over-source-amount guard, and the page has a Calculate Allocations action that runs the engine before posting (simulation). Resolution: env blocker (prerequisite_not_setup — IC setup) + affirmative code evidence → PASS.
CODECod50100.ICRechargeCalculation.al:50-51
if (SourceAmt > 0) and (TotalFixed > SourceAmt + GetRoundingPrecision()) then Error(TotalFixedExceedsSourceErr, TotalFixed, SourceAmt);
CODECod50100.ICRechargeCalculation.al:183-194
if HasPctLines then if Abs(TotalPct - 100) > GetPctTolerance() then Error(PctAllocationNotBalancedErr, TotalPct); if HasFixedLines then begin if TotalFixed > SourceAmt + GetRoundingPrecision() then Error(TotalFixedExceedsSourceErr, TotalFixed, SourceAmt);

if EffectiveCurrCode <> '' then begin ExchRate := GetCurrencyExchangeRate(EffectiveCurrCode, PostingDate, ReqLine."Line No."); ReqLine."Exchange Rate" := ExchRate; if ExchRate <> 0 then ReqLine."Exchange Rate Amount" := Round(ReqLine."Recharge Amount" / ExchRate, GetRoundingPrecision());

Fail

F-06 · weight 3
The approval workflow routes proposals through review statuses, blocks posting of rejected requests and locks approved requests before posting (FR 2 / 7).
Axis: Functional correctness · contributes to 50% of the total score
Codex (GPT-5 CLI)Fail
Council verdict: Fail (1×Fail)
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: status transitions Pending Approval → Approved/Rejected exist; approval-history table written on every decision; PostApproved blocks unless Status=Approved (so rejected requests cannot be posted). 'Locked from edits before posting' is enforced only weakly (no OnModify guard on the table), but Editable=false on several key fields. No L3 UI walkthrough; no env blocker → mandatory UI rule + resolution rule → FAIL.
CODEICRechargeManagement.Codeunit.al:92-132
SubmitForApproval → Status::Pending Approval; Approve → checks current status, blocks creator self-approval over threshold, sets Status::Approved; Reject → sets Status::Rejected, captures Rejection Reason
CODEICRechargeTables.al:192-213
table 50106 "ICR Approval Entry" — Approver ID, Status, Threshold Amount, Decision DateTime, Decision Reason — written on every submit/approve/reject
Claude CodeFail
Council verdict: Fail (1×Fail)
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: Status flow exists (Draft→Validated→Pending Approval→Approved/Rejected→Posted). Approve/Reject guard the current status, and OnModify blocks edits after Posted/Reversed/Closed. However, approved status is reachable without a 'lock' barrier separate from posting — the standard BC ApprovalsMgmt event/handler is NOT used (no call to OnSendDocForApproval). Approval history is the audit log only, not standard Approval Entry. Rejected-cannot-be-posted is enforced (PostRechargeRequest requires Status=Approved). Hand-rolled but covers the spec's basic transitions; missing the request-edit lock between Pending Approval and posting (TestStatusOpen lets you edit while Pending Approval).
CODECod50100.ICRechargeManagement.al:44-94
if ICRechargeHeader.Status <> ...Validated then Error('Document must be validated before submission'); ...Status := ..."Pending Approval"; ...ApproveRechargeRequest: if Status <> Pending Approval then Error... ...RejectRechargeRequest: if Status <> Pending Approval then Error...
CODETab50101.ICRechargeHeader.al:234-238
local procedure TestStatusOpen() begin if Status in [Status::Posted, Status::Reversed, Status::Closed] then Error('Cannot modify document with status %1', Status); end;
DartsFail
Council verdict: Fail (1×Fail)
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: AdvanceStatus blindly walks Draft→Validated→Pending Approval→Approved→Posted with no approval-routing, no Approval Mgmt. event subscription, no threshold check, no approver record, and no Rejected state — the status enum has only 5 values (Draft/Validated/Pending Approval/Approved/Posted), missing the spec's Rejected/Reversed/Closed. There is no approval history table either. The 'approval workflow' is a status flip — not an approval workflow.
CODETab50102.ICRechargeRequestHeader.al:205-234
case Status of Status::Draft: ... Validate(Status, Status::Validated); Status::Validated: Validate(Status, Status::"Pending Approval"); Status::"Pending Approval": Validate(Status, Status::Approved);
CODEEnum50102.ICRechargeRequestStatus.al:1-26
value(0;"Draft") value(1;"Validated") value(2;"Pending Approval") value(3;"Approved") value(4;"Posted")

(3) Standard intercompany inbox/outbox API (ICInboxOutboxMgt) is not used. The 'native intercompany capabilities' integration is fake — direct table writes to BC-system tables, no posting codeunit. attribution: agent_code
L4: Posted Header / Posted Line tables ship with NO OnDelete / OnModify triggers — they are NOT immutable; a user with delete permission can erase posted history. The Audit Log table likewise has no OnDelete guard and no export procedure. Original-vs-corrected linkage is captured via 'Original Document No.' but no comparison view exists. Fails immutability and exportability.

attribution: agent_code
L4: over-source allocation IS blocked and duplicate-posting IS blocked. But several negative cases the spec enumerates are not handled at all — there is no rejected-inbox handling (no inbox module), no reverse-of-already-reversed (no reversal at all), no auto-accept guard for manual-review scenarios, no background-rerun dedup (no JQ), no unauthorized-approval guard (no permission sets). Coverage of the spec's negative list is partial — many failures would not be blocked because the code paths don't exist.

Two outcomes per criterion (PASS / FAIL)

  • Pass — runnable AND verified to meet the spec, OR an unverifiable case the dynamic resolution rule resolves to PASS (env / platform / spec blocker and affirmative L4 code evidence the code is correct).
  • Fail — not runnable, runnable-but-wrong, destructive / insecure, OR an unverifiable case with no affirmative code evidence. Attribution agent_code when the agent owns the defect.

Unverifiable (“inconclusive”) cases are dynamically resolved: PASS only when the blocker is genuinely not the agent’s fault and a code citation proves correctness; otherwise FAIL. The harness re-enforces this rule (a claimed PASS lacking either is downgraded to FAIL, shown as resolution invalid). Axis score = 10 × Σ(weight × passbit) ÷ Σ(weight) (max-scaled at the final step). Council verdict = majority of member votes (ties → FAIL). Total blends Functional 50% / Code quality 10% / Spec 10% / Production readiness 20%; an overall PASS requires the total ≥ 7.0 and every blocking criterion passing.

Verification hierarchy (L1 → L4)

Real Business Central consultants verify in this order; the grader does too:

  • L1 — Find Entries / OData state. The database is the source of truth. After any posting action, query G_LEntries, Customer_Ledger_Entry, posted invoices.
  • L2 — Test codeunits with Library Assert. If the agent shipped tests, the grader runs them.
  • L3 — UI walkthrough via Playwright. User-realistic; captures screenshots for evidence.
  • L4 — Static AL review. Always available backstop, and the source of the code evidence the resolution rule needs. PASS if code invokes the right standard codeunits (e.g. SalesPost.Run, NoSeriesMgt.GetNextNo); FAIL if implementation is a stub, fabricates posting, reinvents BC built-ins, or won’t build.

Cross-validation

Every task is graded by a council of 2–4 independent grader runs (Claude Opus 4.7 + GPT-5.5 ensemble). Second runs of each model are added on tasks where the first-run pair disagreed. The per-criterion verdict is the majority of member PASS/FAIL votes (ties resolve to FAIL); the axis score is then computed deterministically by max-scaling those passbits. Within-model variance is dampened by criterion-level voting.

Scoring axes

The overall score is a weighted blend of up to four axes. Each axis is scored deterministically by max-scaling its criteria’ PASS/FAIL verdicts, then combined — normalized over the axes a task was actually graded on.

  • Functional correctness (50%): does the published extension behave correctly at runtime? Verified via the BC web client and OData queries.
  • Code quality (10%): does the implementation use the right standard BC codeunits and AL idioms rather than stubs, fabricated posting, or reinvented built-ins?
  • Spec understanding (10%): does the submission reflect the requirements as specified — the right entities, fields, workflows and edge cases?
  • Production readiness (20%): would the submission be safe to deploy — status-flow enforcement, field validation, posting integrity, error messages, edge-case handling.
